success_surface: Formula correction with validation loop in Excel spreadsheet manipulation task
effective_behaviors: Conducted comprehensive pre-modification inspection using both pandas for structure analysis and openpyxl for cell-level examination
Programmatic listing of available sheets before attempting access
Identification of multiple formula failure points (invalid day references, array criteria limitations, date boundary logic)
Used SUMPRODUCT wrapper pattern to enable array-based OR criteria in SUMIFS operations
Replaced brittle DATE(year,month,31) pattern with EOMONTH() for robust month-end calculations
Created output directory before saving to prevent runtime errors
Saved modified workbook to new output path without altering source file
Wrote formulas as raw string values to preserve Excel functionality
Reloaded saved file and verified target cell values match expected formulas
Limited modifications to only the specified answer_position range
evidence: Agent loaded workbook twice - once with pandas for shape inspection, once with openpyxl for cell value checking
Explicitly checked wb.sheetnames before accessing Sheet1
Identified three distinct formula issues before proposing solution (Day 31 invalid months, array criteria SUMIFS limitation, missing EOMONTH)
Applied SUMPRODUCT(SUMIFS(...)) pattern to handle range-based criteria from $F$10:$F$14
Changed all three affected cells F6:H6 with relative cell references maintaining formula portability
Created output directory with os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True) before wb.save()
Reloaded output_path with load_workbook() and verified ws[cell].value for each modified cell

memory_items.title: Dual-Library Inspection Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for high-level structure understanding and openpyxl for cell-level details before making modifications
memory_items.generalization: Always inspect the target file comprehensively before editing - combine DataFrame shape analysis with direct cell value checks across all referenced locations

memory_items.title: Array Criteria Formula Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When SUMIFS must evaluate against a list/range of criteria values, wrap it in SUMPRODUCT to handle array evaluation
memory_items.generalization: For OR-style criteria across multiple values from a range, use SUMPRODUCT(SUMIFS(...)) instead of plain SUMIFS which expects single-value comparisons

memory_items.title: Robust Date Boundary Construction
memory_items.lesson: Avoid hardcoded day numbers (like 31) for month-end dates; use EOMONTH() or dynamic date arithmetic
memory_items.generalization: When constructing date ranges based on dynamic reference cells, prefer EOMONTH(date,-1)+1 for start-of-month and EOMONTH(date,0) for end-of-month to handle varying month lengths

memory_items.title: Write-Verify Validation Loop
memory_items.lesson: After saving modifications, reload the output file and verify target cell contents match expectations
memory_items.generalization: Implement a validation pass that loads the newly saved file and confirms critical cell values/formulas are preserved correctly before reporting completion

memory_items.title: Output Path Safety
memory_items.lesson: Ensure the output directory exists before attempting to save files, and write to new paths rather than overwriting sources
memory_items.generalization: Create parent directories with exist_ok flag and always save modifications to a separate output file unless explicitly instructed otherwise

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Optimization Patterns
- **Array Criteria**: When needing to match multiple OR conditions from a criteria range, wrap SUMIFS in SUMPRODUCT to handle array evaluation.
- **Date Boundaries**: Use EOMONTH(date,-1)+1 and EOMONTH(date,0) for robust month-start/month-end calculations instead of hardcoding day numbers.
- **Formula Portability**: Write formulas with relative references where appropriate so they work when copied across similar cells.
- **Write-Verify Loop**: After saving, reload the output file and confirm target cell values/formulas before reporting completion.
